---
title: "Numerical Analysis"
subtitle: "Foundations and Exercises"
author:
  - name: "TANAKA, Kazuaki (田中 一成)"
    email: "k.tanaka@aoni.waseda.jp"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Global Center for Science and Engineering, Waseda University"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../030/custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    chalkboard:
      storage: chalkboardData_9th
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../030/images/image1.png"
      data-background-size: "contain"
      data-background-opacity: "0.15"
---

## Before We Start {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="mission-tip" style="margin-top:0.6em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div class="mission-tip-icon">📥</div>
  <div class="mission-tip-content">
    <strong>Please pull the latest course materials repository.</strong>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.5em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div>
    <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#888; margin-bottom:0.2em;">Already cloned</div>
    <pre style="margin:0; background:#1e1e2e; color:#cdd6f4; border-radius:8px; padding:0.5em 1em; font-size:0.78em; line-height:1.5;"><code>cd materials
git pull</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="background:#f0f4ff; border-left:3px solid #4472C4; border-radius:6px; padding:0.6em 1em; font-size:0.82em;">
  <strong>Today's materials</strong>:
  <table style="margin-top:0.4em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%;">
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>090/9th.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">slides — midterm follow-up and Ex 9.1</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>090/midterm2026_scoring_scheme.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">midterm scoring scheme</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>090/midterm2026_scoring_scheme.html</code></td><td style="color:#555;">rendered scoring scheme</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>
```

## Today's Session {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#fce4ec; border-left:4px solid #c2185b; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em 1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;">
  <div style="font-size:0.82em; color:#880e4f; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.04em;">LECTURE 9</div>
  <div style="font-size:1.05em; margin-top:0.2em;"><strong>Midterm Follow-up — Ex 9.1 Corrections Report</strong></div>
  <div style="font-size:0.84em; color:#455a64; margin-top:0.25em;">
    This is <strong>not</strong> a regular content lecture. Today we review the midterm and explain the correction report assignment.
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:0.8em;">
  <div style="background:#fff8e1; border:1px solid #ffe082; border-radius:10px; padding:0.75em 0.9em;">
    <div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#e65100; font-weight:700;">Today's focus</div>
    <ul style="font-size:0.83em; line-height:1.55; margin:0.4em 0 0 1.2em;">
      <li>reflect on the Midterm Examination 2026</li>
      <li>understand what Ex 9.1 asks you to submit</li>
      <li>learn the workflow: template, AI assistance, render, push</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Accept Ex 9 Repository — Do This First {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#e3f2fd; border-left:4px solid #1565c0; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em 1em; font-size:0.86em; line-height:1.55;">
  <p style="margin:0 0 0.5em 0;">Accept the <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/7XdGlSPT"><strong>Ex 9 GitHub Classroom</strong></a> assignment <strong>as soon as possible</strong> — ideally today, before you start work.</p>
  <ul style="margin:0.2em 0 0 1.2em; padding:0;">
    <li>This creates your personal submission repository with the Ex 9.1 template.</li>
    <li><strong>Your Midterm Examination grading result</strong> and <strong>Ex 9.1 feedback</strong> will be posted as <strong>GitHub Issues</strong> in that repository.</li>
    <li>Click the link early so your repository exists when midterm results are released. Check the <strong>Issues</strong> tab regularly.</li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

## Purpose of Ex 9.1 {.smaller}

**Exercise 9.1 — Midterm Examination Corrections Report** (Individual Work)

Review every midterm problem you answered **incorrectly** or **left blank**, and write a structured correction report that shows:

- what went wrong in your reasoning
- the relevant theory you should have used
- a correct solution with clear steps
- what you learned for future exams

The goal is **understanding**, not just copying model answers.

## Scope — Which Problems? {.smaller}

The midterm covers **Q.1-1 through Q.3-5** and the **Bonus Question**.

For Ex 9.1, include a correction section for **each problem you missed**:

- any sub-question from **Q.1-1** through **Q.3-5** that you answered incorrectly
- the **Bonus Question** if you answered it incorrectly or left it blank / did not answer it

You do **not** need to write corrections for problems you answered correctly.

Use your own exam result to decide which sections to include.

## Files in Your Ex 9 Repository {.smaller}

Your GitHub Classroom repository for Ex 9 provides:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `midterm2026_questions.pdf` | **Primary reference** — full question paper |
| `midterm2026_questions.tex` | Supplementary LaTeX source (copy wording or verify formulas) |
| `ex9-1.qmd` | Correction report template — **edit this file** |

**IMPORTANT:** When copying problem text, verify it against the **PDF**. The PDF is authoritative.

## What to Write for Each Problem {.smaller}

Copy the template in `ex9-1.qmd` once per missed problem. Each section should include:

1. **Problem** — optional or a short quote from the question paper
2. **My Error Analysis** — what you misunderstood or calculated wrongly
3. **Relevant Theory** — definitions, theorems, or formulas needed
4. **Correct Solution** — step-by-step correct reasoning
5. **Key Learning Points** — concise takeaways for future work

After all problem sections, complete the **Overall Reflection** part at the end of the template.

## Workflow — VS Code, Copilot, or Cursor {.smaller}

Recommended editing workflow:

1. Accept the [Ex 9 GitHub Classroom](https://classroom.github.com/a/7XdGlSPT) assignment **as soon as possible**, then clone your repository.
2. Open **`midterm2026_questions.tex`** and **`ex9-1.qmd`** in the same workspace.
3. Ask AI (Copilot Chat, Cursor Composer, etc.) to **copy and format** selected problem statements into the template.
4. **You** write your own error analysis, theory, solutions, and learning points.
5. Compare copied text with **`midterm2026_questions.pdf`** before submitting.

Example prompt (one problem):

```
Open midterm2026_questions.tex and my ex9-1.qmd in this repository.
Find Q.2-3 and insert a new correction section using the template structure.
Copy only the problem statement. Leave placeholders for My Error Analysis,
Relevant Theory, Correct Solution, and Key Learning Points — I will fill
those in myself. Preserve all formulas when converting LaTeX to Quarto Markdown.
```

More prompt examples are in `ex9-1.qmd`.

## Exercise 9.1 — Midterm Corrections Report {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Format:</strong> Individual Work
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Repository:</strong> Ex 9 — <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/7XdGlSPT">GitHub Classroom</a>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex9-1.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: 6/18</strong>
</div>

Markdown + LaTeX only — **no Python code** is required for this exercise.

Install [Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) if needed:

```
quarto check
```

## Render and Submit {.smaller .exercise-slide}

Render your report:

```
quarto render ex9-1.qmd
```

This creates `ex9-1.html` and the `ex9-1_files/` directory.

Then commit and push:

```
git add ex9-1.qmd ex9-1.html ex9-1_files
git commit -m "Submit Ex 9.1"
git push
```

**IMPORTANT:**

- Push before the deadline; the **last commit before the deadline** is graded.
- After pushing, open your repository on GitHub and confirm the rendered HTML is visible in the browser.

## Grading and Feedback {.smaller}

- Accept the [Ex 9 GitHub Classroom](https://classroom.github.com/a/7XdGlSPT) link **early** so your repository exists before midterm results are released.
- Your **Midterm Examination grading result** will be posted as a **GitHub Issue** in this repository.
- Your **Ex 9.1 grading result and feedback** will also be posted as a **GitHub Issue** in the same repository.
- Check the **Issues** tab regularly — this is the standard feedback channel for both the midterm result and Ex 9.1.

## Ex 9.1 — Scoring Formula {.smaller}

Your Ex 9.1 score combines your midterm result with your correction report:

$$S = \sum_i \left( S_{1,i} + C_i \left( S_{2,i} - S_{1,i} \right) \right)$$

```{=html}
<table style="margin:0.45em 0 0.6em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%; font-size:0.86em; line-height:1.45;">
  <tr style="background:#f0f4ff;">
    <th style="padding:0.3em 0.55em; text-align:left; border:1px solid #c5cae9; width:18%;">Symbol</th>
    <th style="padding:0.3em 0.55em; text-align:left; border:1px solid #c5cae9;">Meaning</th>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><em>i</em></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;">Problem index — includes the <strong>Bonus Question</strong> when applicable</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><em>S</em><sub>1,<em>i</em></sub></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;">Your <strong>original midterm score</strong> for problem <em>i</em></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><em>S</em><sub>2,<em>i</em></sub></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><strong>Full score</strong> for problem <em>i</em> (maximum points available)</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><em>C</em><sub><em>i</em></sub></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;">Quality factor from your <strong>correction report</strong> for problem <em>i</em> (see next slide)</td>
  </tr>
</table>
```

## Ex 9.1 — Correction Quality (C_i) {.smaller}

$C_i$ reflects how well you corrected each problem. Higher $C_i$ recovers more of the gap $S_{2,i} - S_{1,i}$.

```{=html}
<table style="margin:0.45em 0; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%; font-size:0.86em; line-height:1.45;">
  <tr style="background:#f0f4ff;">
    <th style="padding:0.3em 0.55em; text-align:left; border:1px solid #c5cae9;">Problem type</th>
    <th style="padding:0.3em 0.55em; text-align:left; border:1px solid #c5cae9;">Full-credit quality</th>
    <th style="padding:0.3em 0.55em; text-align:left; border:1px solid #c5cae9;">Excellent (upper limit)</th>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;">Regular problems<br><span style="color:#555;">Q.1-1 through Q.3-5</span></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><strong>C<sub>i</sub> = 0.4</strong></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><strong>C<sub>i</sub> = 0.5</strong></td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><strong>Bonus Question</strong><br><span style="color:#555;">stricter grading (bonus problem)</span></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd;"><strong>C<sub>i</sub> = 0.2</strong><br><span style="color:#555;">ordinary full-credit-quality work</span></td>
    <td style="padding:0.25em 0.55em; border:1px solid #ddd; color:#777;">—</td>
  </tr>
</table>
```

## Academic Integrity and AI Use {.smaller}

**Permitted AI assistance:**

- formatting Markdown and LaTeX
- copying problem statements from `midterm2026_questions.tex` into the template
- syntax help for mathematical notation

**You must do yourself:**

- decide **which** problems you missed (do not ask AI to invent your exam result)
- write your own error analysis, correct solutions, and reflections
- verify that copied problem text matches the PDF

The report must reflect **your own understanding**.

## Checklist Before You Submit {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#e8f5e9; border-left:4px solid #2e7d32; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em 1em; font-size:0.84em; line-height:1.55;">
  <ul style="margin:0.2em 0 0 1.2em; padding:0;">
    <li>I included every midterm problem I answered incorrectly (Q.1-1 through Q.3-5).</li>
    <li>If I missed the Bonus Question, I included a correction section for it.</li>
    <li>Each problem section has My Error Analysis, Relevant Theory, Correct Solution, and Key Learning Points.</li>
    <li>I completed the Overall Reflection section.</li>
    <li>Problem statements match <code>midterm2026_questions.pdf</code>.</li>
    <li>I ran <code>quarto render ex9-1.qmd</code> successfully.</li>
    <li>I pushed <code>ex9-1.qmd</code>, <code>ex9-1.html</code>, and <code>ex9-1_files/</code>.</li>
    <li>I opened my GitHub repo in a browser and confirmed the HTML renders correctly.</li>
  </ul>
</div>
```

## Assignments and Preparation {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="assign-list">
  <div class="assign-card">
    <div class="assign-num">1</div>
    <div class="assign-body">
      <p>Accept the <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/7XdGlSPT"><strong>Ex 9 GitHub Classroom</strong></a> link <strong>as soon as possible</strong> to get your repository.</p>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> both your Midterm Examination grading result and Ex 9.1 feedback are posted as <strong>GitHub Issues</strong> in your repo — check the <strong>Issues</strong> tab regularly</li>
        <li><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> render <code>ex9-1.qmd</code> and push the rendered output together with <code>ex9-1_files/</code></li>
        <li><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> after pushing, open your GitHub repository in a browser and confirm the rendered output is visible</li>
        <li>Use AI only as permitted — your analysis and solutions must be your own work</li>
        <li><strong>Deadline:</strong> <span class="assign-due">6/18</span></li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Need Help? {.smaller}

- Mattermost: <https://class.s-top.dev/numerical-analysis-2026/channels/question>
- Materials repo: <https://github.com/waseda-num-analysis-2026/materials>
- Ex 9 template README: see your GitHub Classroom repository after acceptance
